description_long: This object consists of two medieval-style swords crossed in an 'X' formation. Each sword features a straight, double-edged blade with a slightly mottled, metallic texture, suggesting forged steel. The hilts are simple, with a short, straight crossguard and a round pommel, both rendered in a silvery metal. The handles are cylindrical and appear to be made of wood, with a smooth, brown finish. The overall design is utilitarian, lacking any visible ornamentation or embellishments. The swords are positioned closely together, with their blades overlapping at the center.
description: A pair of crossed, straight-bladed medieval swords with wooden handles and simple metal guards and pommels.
description_view_0: Front view showing both swords crossed, with full visibility of the blades, guards, handles, and pommels.
description_view_1: Side view showing the thin profile of the swords, with blades and handles aligned and minimal visible detail.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the crossed blades and all major features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin edge profile of the swords with little surface detail visible.
attribute_handle_design: straight, cylindrical, smooth wooden handle with round metal pommel
